findings.title: Regex does not match on non-string/non-bytes header values (e.g. integers), causing TypeError instead of InvalidHeader
findings.detail:
check_header_validity calls pat.match(value) without checking that value is actually a str or bytes first. If a caller passes a numeric value (e.g. {'Content-Length': 16}), pat.match() will raise a TypeError instead of the expected InvalidHeader. Many users legitimately pass integer header values. The function should either coerce value to a string before checking, or catch TypeError and raise InvalidHeader, or explicitly handle non-string types.

Example: requests.get(url, headers={'X-Retry': 3}) → TypeError: expected string or bytes-like object.

Suggested fix: add a guard like
  if not isinstance(value, (str, bytes)):
      value = str(value)
before the pattern match (or decide that non-str/bytes values are always valid and return early).

findings.title: Header name (key) is never validated — only the value is checked
findings.detail: check_header_validity only validates the header value, but not the header name. A malicious header name containing \r\n (e.g. {'foo\r\nX-Injected: bar': 'value'}) would pass through unchecked and could still result in header injection depending on the underlying HTTP layer. Both name and value should be validated.

findings.title: Regex allows values starting with \r or \n (bare carriage-return or linefeed at start)
Headers added after prepare_headers() (e.g. by prepare_body, prepare_auth, prepare_cookies) are never validated
findings.detail:
check_header_validity is only called during prepare_headers(), which processes the user-supplied headers dict. However, several other prepare_* methods append headers directly to self.headers without validation:
- prepare_body() sets 'Content-Length', 'Transfer-Encoding', 'Content-Type'
- prepare_auth() can set arbitrary auth headers via the auth callable
- prepare_cookies() sets 'Cookie'

Of these, the auth callable is the most dangerous: a custom auth object that injects \r\n into a header name or value it sets will bypass the check entirely. The validation should also be applied (or the auth path should be independently checked).

This is an architectural concern, but the PR description itself raises it — the check is intentionally placed in prepare_headers. At minimum, the limitation should be documented.

findings.title: Missing blank line before function definition (PEP 8)
findings.detail: There is no blank line between `to_native_string`'s closing `return out` and the module-level regex constants / `check_header_validity` function. PEP 8 requires two blank lines before top-level definitions. The surrounding code consistently follows this convention.

findings.title: New tests require a live httpbin server — should use unit tests against PreparedRequest instead
findings.detail: test_header_validation, test_header_no_return_chars, and test_header_no_leading_space all accept `httpbin` as a fixture and make real HTTP requests. Since the validation is done in prepare_headers() before any network activity, these tests don't need a live server. Using `requests.Request(...).prepare()` or `PreparedRequest().prepare_headers()` directly would make the tests faster, more isolated, and not dependent on network availability. The existing test test_header_keys_are_native already demonstrates this pattern correctly.

findings.title: test_header_no_leading_space: tab character test uses spaces, not an actual tab
findings.detail: The comment says '# Test for tab' but `headers_tab = {'foo': '   bar'}` uses three space characters, not a tab (\t). A tab is also whitespace and should be rejected, but it is not tested here. The comment is misleading.

findings.title: test_header_validation uses str.encode(u'fbbq') which always produces b'fbbq', not a unicode test
findings.detail: The test includes `'qux': str.encode(u'fbbq')` as a 'valid' header value. This encodes to bytes b'fbbq' rather than testing a unicode string. The comment says 'fbbq' but it reads oddly. More importantly, a tab-indented value or a value beginning with a space is never tested as valid to establish boundaries. The test for valid headers is minimal — only the invalid cases are well covered.

findings.title: Trailing whitespace on import line
findings.detail: Line 30 has a trailing space after the comma on `iter_slices, guess_json_utf, super_len, to_native_string, `. This should be removed.

findings.title: Missing space after comma in test_header_validation signature
findings.detail: `def test_header_validation(self,httpbin):` is missing a space after the comma, inconsistent with PEP 8 and the rest of the file's style.
